question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicServiceImpl is a @Named singleton with a shared topicRepository instance to cause a race condition where concurrent findById() calls for the same ID interleave at the repository layer, potentially returning inconsistent Topic objects that cause gossip transactions to be routed to incorrect topic configurations?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of input range validation in findById() to pass an EntityId with id=Long.MIN_VALUE (-9223372036854775808), causing topicRepository.findById() to query for a negative primary key that may cause unexpected behavior in the JPA/Hibernate layer, potentially throwing an uncaught exception that disrupts gossip transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicServiceImpl.findById() does not check whether the Topic's submitKey is set, to discover topics with no submit key restriction (open topics) via the REST API, and then flood those topics with gossip transactions to exhaust network bandwidth and prevent legitimate transactions from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TopicController calling findById() alongside entityService.findById() and customFeeService.findById() in sequence (three separate DB queries per request) to amplify the DB load by a factor of 3x compared to a single-query endpoint, making DoS attacks 3x more effective at preventing gossip transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of pagination or result limiting in findById() to cause the TopicRepository (CrudRepository<Topic, Long>) to load an unexpectedly large Topic entity with large adminKey, submitKey, or feeExemptKeyList byte arrays into memory, causing heap pressure that degrades gossip transaction processing performance?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityIdNumParameter regex allowing up to 12 digits for num ('^((\\d{1,4})\\.)?((\\d{1,5})\\.)?(\\d{1,12})$') to craft a topic ID with num=999999999999 (12 nines), which when encoded with non-zero shard/realm produces a negative long, causing findById() to issue a negative-key DB query that may behave differently from positive-key queries and leak timing information about gossip topics?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicServiceImpl has no audit logging of findById() calls to perform reconnaissance of the gossip topic infrastructure without leaving any trace, enabling the attacker to silently map all active HCS topics used for transaction gossip before launching a suppression attack?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the deterministic EntityId encoding (shard=0,realm=0,num=N encodes to N) to predict the encoded IDs of newly created gossip topics by monitoring the sequential allocation of topic numbers, and preemptively flood findById() for those IDs before the topics are fully initialized, causing race conditions in topic setup that prevent gossip transactions from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of connection pooling limits specific to the topic service to starve other services (entityService, customFeeService) of DB connections by flooding findById() with requests, since all three services share the same DB connection pool, preventing the full topic resolution pipeline needed for gossip transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that EntityNotFoundException thrown by findById() propagates up through TopicController without being caught at the service layer, causing the exception to bubble through the Spring MVC exception handler and potentially exposing internal stack trace information that reveals gossip topic infrastructure details?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CrudRepository.findById() method's reliance on JPA entity manager to cause a second-level cache poisoning attack, where a stale Topic entity (with outdated submitKey or adminKey) is cached in the JPA L2 cache after a findById() call, causing subsequent gossip transactions to use the stale key configuration and be rejected by the network?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maximum request body size or query complexity limit on the topic ID path parameter to send an extremely long numeric string that passes the regex but causes Long.parseLong() to throw NumberFormatException, which if not properly handled could cause an unhandled 500 error that disrupts gossip transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicServiceImpl.findById() does not validate the Topic's expirationTimestamp, allowing the attacker to discover expired topics via the REST API and submit gossip transactions to those expired topics, which will be silently dropped by the network, effectively preventing those transactions from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findById() returns the full Topic domain object (including adminKey, submitKey, feeExemptKeyList, feeScheduleKey byte arrays) without field-level access control, to extract the submitKey of a gossip topic and use it to craft valid-looking but malicious ConsensusSubmitMessage transactions that flood the topic and prevent legitimate gossip transactions from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of IP-based rate limiting on the /api/v1/topics/{id} endpoint backed by findById() to perform a distributed amplification attack from multiple source IPs, each sending requests at just below any per-IP threshold, collectively overwhelming the DB with topic lookup queries and preventing gossip transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityId encoding where shard=0,realm=0 produces id=num to craft a topic ID of num=1 (the first valid topic), call findById() to retrieve its full Topic metadata including runningHash and sequenceNumber fields, and use this information to forge or replay gossip transactions that appear to be legitimate continuations of the topic's message sequence?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the deterministic relationship between topic num and encoded ID (for shard=0,realm=0) to perform a binary search enumeration of all valid topic IDs in O(log N) requests, then use the discovered topic list to identify which topics are used for critical gossip transactions and target them with a focused DoS attack?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the EntityId Caffeine cache uses the encoded long as the cache key (not the shard.realm.num string), so two different string representations that encode to the same long will share a cache entry, to perform a cache collision attack that causes findById() to return a cached Topic for a different logical topic ID, misdirecting gossip transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/TopicServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findById(EntityId id)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TopicServiceImpl is final (cannot be subclassed or proxied with AOP) to bypass any AOP-based security interceptors that might be applied to non-final service classes, allowing unrestricted access to findById() without rate limiting or authentication checks that would otherwise prevent gossip topic enumeration?
